title: fix: redeploy shared package app consumers
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Redeploy registered app subdomains when `packages/sdk/**` or `packages/ui/**` changes by detecting apps that declare those package dependencies.
- Keep direct `apps/**` deploy behavior and `workflow_dispatch` unchanged.
- Move package-consumer detection into `.github/scripts/detect-package-consumers.js` so it can be linted and tested outside the workflow YAML.
- Add a fixture test for changed-files in -> app-list out, including missing `package.json` and non-package app changes.
- Run `.github/scripts/*.test.js` in the existing Biome Check workflow whenever `.github/scripts/*.js` changes.
- Keep the script minimal: CLI input is only the workflow-provided `CHANGED` env var; tests use the exported function.

Checks:
- `PATH=/opt/homebrew/bin:$PATH npx biome check --write .github/scripts/detect-package-consumers.js .github/scripts/detect-package-consumers.test.js .github/workflows/biome-check.yml .github/workflows/app-deploy-automatic.yml`
- `PATH=/opt/homebrew/bin:$PATH node --test .github/scripts/*.test.js`
- `git diff --check`
- Ruby YAML parse for changed workflow files
- Simulated package-change detection returns `model-monitor`, `playground`, `react`

gist.headline: Ripple effect deployments
gist.blurb: Apps now automatically redeploy when their shared UI or SDK dependencies are updated. Keeping the whole garden perfectly in sync!
gist.summary: Updated the automatic deployment workflow to detect and redeploy apps whenever their shared dependencies in `packages/sdk` or `packages/ui` are modified.
gist.impact: Ensures that downstream applications always reflect the latest changes from shared internal packages without requiring manual redeploys.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee watering a central 'package' plant that causes a network of smaller 'app' plants to bloom simultaneously, representing the automated dependency redeployments.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot watering a large central glowing code-plant, causing several smaller connected plants in lime green (#ecf874) pots to bloom simultaneously. A chunky robot with a CRT monitor head watches happily, screen displaying a network graph. Soft pastel gradient background, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley.
